figures.caption: Table 1: Five implementation failure modes in the v1 PPO baseline and their targeted fixes applied in v2.

Table 4: Ablation study. Each variant removes exactly one fix from v3. Win rate vs. random at episode 12,000 (seed 42 unless noted). ∆ is computed relative to the 10-seed mean (77.1%). †Seed 42 v3 full achieved 93.9%; seed-matched ∆ = −6.8 pp.

Figure 5: Training diagnostic curves (mean ± std over 10 seeds). Entropy decreases as the policy specialises; explained variance rises toward 1.0, confirming the critic learns accurate TD-λ return estimates.

Figure 1: The Territory Paint Wars environment on a 10×10 grid. Light pink/green tiles are owned by each agent; dark pink/dark green tiles are locked and cannot be reclaimed; grey tiles are neutral. Agents (spheres) paint the tile they occupy each step.
Figure 3: Competitive overfitting in v2 self-play. Win rate vs. random rises then collapses, while self-play win rate gives no warning. Opponent mixing (v3) prevents the collapse.

Figure 4: Learning curves: rolling-100 Pink self-play win rate for all 10 seeds (v3, opponent mixing). Despite high rolling variance (co-adaptive oscillation), the cumulative win rate climbs steadily for all seeds, confirming persistent learning.

Figure 2: Win-rate progression across all versions. The red bar (v2 at ep 12,000) exposes competitive overfitting; the blue bar (v3 mean, 10 seeds) shows the recovery via opponent mixing. Error bar on v3 mean shows ±1 SD across seeds.
Table 2: PPO+GAE v3 hyperparameters. Values are identical for both agents.

Table 3: 10-seed convergence study. Cumul. WR: Pink cumulative self-play win rate at ep 12,000. vs. Random: generalisation win rate against a uniformly-random opponent (100 games for seeds 42–49; 500 games for seeds 50–51).
